Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Negative existential predicate with possessed noun phrase
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Someone lacks compassion or empathy toward others.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Ja' mu xyak' smuk' ta viniketik.
Usage Contexts: Family disputes, moral criticism, community leadership
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Linked to the Tzotzil concept of ch'ulel as spiritual consciousness and emotional balance.
First Example Sentence: Li mol antsetike laj yalbeic: “Mu xch'ulelaluk ta yo'on li jchopone.”
Second Example Sentence: Mu xa xch'ulelaluk ta yo'on ta spaseluk smelol li alale.
Third Example Sentence: Ta sna li bankile mu xch'ulelaluk ta yo'on ta sventa li me'onetike.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Fixed verbal clause with directional complement
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To disappear suddenly or avoid responsibility.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Commonly used in highland communities when speaking about unreliable individuals.
First Example Sentence: Li kereme laj xch'ay ta sat ta ora to laj yich' ta muk' li tak'in.
Second Example Sentence: Ta pasel k'op li j-ilol xch'ay ta sat.
Third Example Sentence: Mu xa xvinikajic li buch'u xch'ay ta sat ti ora li smelol oy.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Existential clause with possessed nominal phrase
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Someone has become arrogant or conceited.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Reflects communal expectations of humility in Tzotzil society.
First Example Sentence: Desde laj xa yich' yabtel, oy xa smuk' ta sjol.
Second Example Sentence: Li ach' ixime oy xa smuk' ta sjol ta stojol li yan antsetike.
Third Example Sentence: Mu me xapas avo'on jech chijc'ot ta oy smuk' ta ajol.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Motion verb phrase with accompaniment marker
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: To act recklessly without thinking of consequences.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Traditional metaphor comparing impulsive people to leaves carried by wind.
First Example Sentence: Li vinike chbat xchi'uk ik' ta spasel li lo'il.
Second Example Sentence: Mu xavil ti chbat xchi'uk ik' li ach' kereme.
Third Example Sentence: Ta jna'el bats'i k'op mu xtun ti chbat xchi'uk ik'.

Syntactic Structure & Grammatical Form: Completed verbal construction with relational noun
Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Someone spoke sincerely and from the heart.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Strongly associated with oral reconciliation traditions in Chamula communities.
First Example Sentence: Li me'ile laj yal ta o'ontonal ta stojol li snich'nabe.
Second Example Sentence: Ta tsobajel laj yal ta o'ontonal li bankile.

Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: A person is inexperienced or naïve about the real world.
Non-Idiomatic Equivalent (Plain, Neutral: Mu to xna' spasel smelol kuxlejal.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Balumil metaphorically represents worldly experience across Maya traditions.
First Example Sentence: Li alale mu to xna' xch'unel balumil.
Second Example Sentence: Ta slumal Mexico ep to li buch'u mu xna' xch'unel balumil.
Third Example Sentence: Mu me xapas mantal jech yu'un mu to xana' xch'unel balumil.

Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Someone is emotionally troubled or hiding concerns.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Rooted in the Maya understanding of the soul as a seat of memory and emotion.
First Example Sentence: Ta xch'ulel oy k'op li antse yu'un li chamel.
Second Example Sentence: K'alal mu xava'i ti ta xch'ulel oy k'op.
Third Example Sentence: Li bankile ta xch'ulel oy k'op pero mu xyal.

Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Someone abandoned responsibility or withdrew support.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Derived from physical imagery of carrying burdens on one’s back.
First Example Sentence: Li yajval laj xa xkuch ta pat li yabtel.
Second Example Sentence: Ta yutil sna laj xa xkuch ta pat li alale.
Third Example Sentence: Mu xtun ti laj xa xkuch ta pat ti ach' kuxlejale.

Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Someone only talks but never acts.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Widely used in informal community interaction.
First Example Sentence: Li jchi'iltike ja' no'ox ta sti' ta sventa li lum.
Second Example Sentence: Mu xch'unic li vinik yu'un ja' no'ox ta sti'.
Third Example Sentence: Ja' no'ox ta sti' pero mu xcolta ta yabtel.

Figurative Meaning (Contextualized Defin: Someone is angry, embarrassed, or emotionally overwhelmed.
Cultural Origin or Historical Reference: Color symbolism is important in Maya emotional metaphors.
First Example Sentence: Tzajal xa sat li kereme ta stojol li ach' antse.
Second Example Sentence: Ta pasel k'op tzajal xa sat li bankile.
Third Example Sentence: K'alal laj yalbe talel li lo'il, tzajal xa sat.
